name: Indrik
description: The Indrik is a wondrous beast from Slavic mythology, often described as the 'king of beasts'. It is a powerful and benevolent creature, believed to possess immense strength and wisdom. Unlike many mythical creatures, the Indrik is not malevolent; instead, it is seen as a protector of the animal kingdom and a source of natural balance. It lives in the Holy Mountain, a sacred place at the center of the world, from where it derives its power. Some accounts depict it as a bull-like creature with a single horn, while others describe it as a horse or a deer. The Indrik is said to possess the ability to dig underground, creating rivers and springs with its horn. It is a creature of immense size and power, capable of shaking the earth with its movements. Its role is to maintain the cosmic order and protect the world from chaos. Though rarely seen by humans, its influence is felt throughout the natural world. The Indrik is a symbol of strength, wisdom, and the interconnectedness of all living things. It is also considered as a master of the underground, responsible for the flow of underground waters.
subtitle: King of Beasts, Earth Shaker
appearance: The Indrik is described variably, but often as a massive, bull-like creature or a giant rhinoceros. It is typically depicted with a single, large horn protruding from its forehead, which is used for digging and creating underground waterways. Its body is immense and powerful, capable of causing tremors in the earth. Some accounts also mention deer-like features or a horse-like appearance. The color of its hide is rarely specified, but it is generally assumed to be a natural, earthy tone.
habitat: The Indrik is said to reside on the Holy Mountain, a mythical location at the center of the world. This mountain is a sacred and powerful place, serving as the source of the Indrik's strength and connection to the earth. From this central location, it influences the entire world, maintaining the balance of nature.
behavior: The Indrik is a benevolent and wise creature, acting as a protector of the animal kingdom and a guardian of natural balance. It is not aggressive towards humans unless provoked or if the natural order is threatened. Its primary function is to maintain the cosmic equilibrium, and it will use its immense power to do so. The Indrik is generally solitary and rarely interacts directly with the world above ground, preferring to remain in its subterranean realm.

keyMyths.title: Creation of Rivers
keyMyths.description: The Indrik is believed to create underground rivers and springs by digging with its horn. These waterways are essential for the sustenance of life and are a testament to the Indrik's power over the earth.

sources.author: W.R.S. Ralston
sources.description: A collection of Russian folk tales, including mentions of the Indrik.
sources.text: A collection of Russian folk tales, including mentions of the Indrik.
